title: helianthus grosseserratus
intro: Helianthus grosseserratus, also known as the rough-serrated sunflower, is a perennial plant species native to North America, characterized by its large, daisy-like flowers and coarse, serrated leaves. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, with medicinal properties including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Helianthus grosseserratus include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for consumption, or used topically as a poultice or salve to benefit from its medicinal properties.
